name: MacTutor
biography:
Fran횄짠ois Bruhat 's parents were Georges Bruhat, who was assistant director of the 횄혟cole Normale Sup횄짤rieure in Paris, and Berthe Hubert. Fran횄짠ois attended the Lyc횄짤e Henri IV in Paris, going on to prepare for university entry at the Lyc횄짤e Saint-Louis, also in Paris. From there he entered the 횄혟cole Normale Sup횄짤rieure where he studied for his Agr횄짤g횄짤 de mathematique. He was appointed to the National Centre for Scientific research in 1952 and he worked there until 1955 .
Bruhat was among the first to understand the importance of distributions, as introduced by Laurent Schwartz , in the theory of Lie groups and their representations and his doctoral thesis was devoted to these topics. He published Sur les repr횄짤sentations induites des groupes de Lie 창혫혟 ( On induced representations of Lie groups ) in 1953 on this topic and continued his investigations in Irr횄짤ductibilit횄짤 des repr횄짤sentations induites des groupes de Lie 창혫혟 ( Irreducibility of induced representations of Lie groups ) which was published in the following year. This paper considered induced representations which Bruhat then applied in his next paper Repr횄짤sentations induites des groupes de Lie semi-simples complexes 창혫혟 ( Induced representations of complex semi-simple Lie groups ) (1954) to a complex semi-simple Lie group none of whose simple factor groups is one of the exceptional simple Lie groups. In the same year his paper Repr횄짤sentations induites des groupes de Lie semi-simples r횄짤els 창혫혟 ( Induced representations of real semi-simple Lie groups ) appeared which dealt with the case of a real semi-simple Lie group. In a major 108 page paper Sur les repr횄짤sentations induites des groupes de Lie 창혫혟 ( On induced representations of Lie groups ) (1956) Bruhat gave a new theory in which he dealt with more restricted groups but was able to handle more general representations of them.
After spending three years at the National Centre for Scientific Research, Bruhat was appointed as Ma횄짰tre de Conf횄짤rences at the Faculty of Science in Nancy in 1955 . He was later promoted to professor of mathematics at Nancy. Bruhat published two papers which resulted from a collaboration with Henri Cartan in 1957 . These papers Sur la structure des sous-ensembles analytiques r횄짤els 창혫혟 ( On the structure of real analytic subsets ) and Sur les composantes irr횄짤ductibles d'un sous-ensemble analytique-r횄짤el 창혫혟 ( On the irreducible components of an analytical real-subset ) deal with real analytic manifolds with singularities. Bruhat continued his study of real analytic manifolds in a collaboration with Whitney . A course of lecture given by Bruhat in India in 1958 were published as Lectures on Lie groups and representations of locally compact groups and reissued in 1968 . The lectures are in three parts. The first covers elementary properties of Lie groups, the second covers the general theory of measures on a locally compact group and representation in general, while the final part contains a construction of the continuous sum of Hilbert spaces, decomposition of a unitary representation into a continuous sum of irreducible representations and the Plancherel formula.
In 1959 Bruhat lectured on Lie groups and Lie algebras at Recife in Brazil and his lecture notes were published as Alg횄짤bres de Lie et groupes de Lie : R횄짤sum횄짤 des ele횄짠ons in the same year. Another lecture course, this time given in India, was published as Lectures on some aspects of p-adic analysis in 1963 . By this time Bruhat had an appointment in Paris, having been appointed to the Faculty of Science there in 1961 . He was appointed to Universit횄짤 de Paris VII in 1970 and he held this post until 1989 . From 1976 to 1981 he was vice-president of the university.
Together with Jacques Tits , Bruhat developed the theory of simple algebraic groups over local fields. They published a number of notes containing their results in this area but a detailed account of the theory of reductive algebraic groups over local fields appeared in their joint 250 page paper Groupes r횄짤ductifs sur un corps local published in 1972 .
Finally we should mention that Bruhat was a member of Bourbaki being a third generation member along with Armand Borel , Alexandre Grothendieck , Pierre Cartier , Serge Lang , and John Tate .
Among the honours that Bruhat received were that he was made an Officer of the L횄짤gion d'Honneur and the national order of merit. He was also made Commandeur des Palmes Acad횄짤miques.
